content.problemStatement:
Read problem statements in Vietnamese,Chefland has 222 different types of coconut, type AAA and type BBB. Type AAA contains only xax_axa​ milliliters of coconut water and type BBB contains only xbx_bxb​ grams of coconut pulp. Chef's nutritionist has advised him to consume XaX_aXa​ milliliters of coconut water and XbX_bXb​ grams of coconut pulp every week in the summer. Find the total number of coconuts (type AAA + type BBB) that Chef should buy each week to keep himself active in the hot weather.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, four integers xax_axa​, xbx_bxb​, XaX_aXa​, XbX_bXb​.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the answer to the problem.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers: xa xb Xa Xb.
- xa: milliliters of water in one type‑AAA coconut.
- xb: grams of pulp in one type‑BBB coconut.
- Xa: total milliliters of water Chef must consume each week.
- Xb: total grams of pulp Chef must consume each week.
It is guaranteed that xa divides Xa and xb divides Xb.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the total number of coconuts Chef has to buy (type‑AAA plus type‑BBB).

solution.approach:
For each test case the answer is simply the sum of two independent quotients:
- Number of type‑AAA coconuts = Xa / xa
- Number of type‑BBB coconuts = Xb / xb
Because the divisibility guarantees are given, integer division yields exact counts. Iterate over all test cases and output the sum.
The algorithm is O(1) per test case and uses O(1) additional memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        xa = int(next(it))
        xb = int(next(it))
        Xa = int(next(it))
        Xb = int(next(it))
        out_lines.append(str(Xa // xa + Xb // xb))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
